key_insight: A transfer-learning + hybrid Cross-Entropy/Lovász optimization on BiSeNetV2 greatly improves reflective seam segmentation without adding model complexity.
engineering_relevance: This offers a lightweight, real-time perception upgrade for robotic welding in construction where glare, reflections, and thin seams commonly break segmentation.
method: Fine-tune BiSeNetV2 with transfer learning instead of enlarging the network.
Use a hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss to stabilize learning on difficult reflective seams.
Compare against OHEM baseline and other segmenters (DeepLabV3+, UNet, SegFormer) under harsh welding conditions.
evidence_signal: The proposed method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline while keeping FLOPs, parameters, and speed unchanged.
watch_item: Results are promising, but deployment still depends on whether the trained model generalizes across different weld materials, camera setups, and lighting conditions.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
